examples.title: Pseudocode: Pseudocolor Processing
examples.description: Pseudocolor processing using lookup table method
examples.code:
ALGORITHM PseudocolorProcessing
INPUT: grayscaleImage, colorMap
OUTPUT: pseudocolorImage

BEGIN
  FOR EACH pixel IN grayscaleImage
    intensity = GET_INTENSITY(pixel)
    rgbValue = LOOKUP(colorMap, intensity)
    ASSIGN_RGB(pixel, rgbValue)
  END FOR
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the lookup table method for pseudocolor processing. It iterates over each pixel in the grayscale image, looks up the corresponding RGB value in the color map, and assigns it to the output pixel.

examples.title: Python: Pseudocolor Processing
examples.description: Pseudocolor processing using lookup table method
examples.code:
import numpy as np
from PIL import Image

# Define a color map
 color_map = np.array([(0, 0, 255), (0, 255, 0), (255, 0, 0)])

# Load a grayscale image
img_gray = Image.open('image.jpg').convert('L')

# Create a pseudocolor image
img_pseudo = Image.new('RGB', img_gray.size)

# Iterate over each pixel and assign RGB value from color map
for x in range(img_gray.size[0]):
  for y in range(img_gray.size[1]):
    intensity = img_gray.getpixel((x, y))
    rgb_value = color_map[intensity // 85]
    img_pseudo.putpixel((x, y), rgb_value)

# Save the pseudocolor image
img_pseudo.save('pseudocolor_image.jpg')
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates the lookup table method for pseudocolor processing. It loads a grayscale image, creates a pseudocolor image, and assigns RGB values from a predefined color map to each pixel based on its intensity.
